ClickUp vs Fireflies.ai: Which Is Better?

Choosing between ClickUp and Fireflies.ai? Here's a side-by-side comparison to help you decide.

Quick verdict: Choose ClickUp if you need small-teams or agencies. Choose Fireflies.ai if you prioritize remote-teams or sales-teams.

ClickUp

PricingFree / $7/mo Unlimited / $12/mo Business
Rating★★★★ 4.2/5

Key Features

  • 15+ project views including list, board, Gantt, calendar, timeline, and workload
  • Docs with collaborative editing and nested pages
  • Whiteboards for visual brainstorming and diagramming
  • Goals with target tracking and progress roll-ups
  • Automations for repetitive task management workflows
  • Built-in email, chat, and clip recording

Pros

  • Feature count is honestly ridiculous — 15+ views, Docs, Whiteboards, Goals, Mind Maps, Clips, Chat, Email
  • Unlimited plan at $7/mo is excellent value given everything included
  • Custom fields and automations are powerful enough to replicate most workflows you'd build in dedicated tools
  • Multiple view types (list, board, Gantt, calendar, timeline, workload) cover almost every use case
  • Dashboards with widgets give a real-time picture of progress across multiple projects

Cons

  • Can feel bloated; the sheer number of features makes it overwhelming for new users
  • Learning curve is steep — expect a week or two of 'where do I find X' before it clicks
  • Performance degrades noticeably with large workspaces (500+ tasks, multiple custom fields)
  • Mobile app is functional but slow and lacks many desktop features
  • Some features feel half-baked compared to dedicated tools (the chat is no Slack, the docs are no Notion)
Try ClickUp →

Fireflies.ai

PricingFree / $10/mo Pro / $19/mo Business
Rating★★★★ 4.2/5

Key Features

  • Automatic meeting recording and transcription across Zoom, Meet, Teams
  • AI-powered meeting summaries with key decisions and action items
  • Searchable meeting history across your entire team
  • Soundbites for clipping and sharing specific moments from recordings
  • Conversation intelligence for sales teams and deal analysis
  • Integrations with Notion, Slack, Asana, Salesforce, and HubSpot

Pros

  • Auto-joins meetings and records them without anyone needing to remember or click anything
  • Transcription accuracy is good for clean audio, though it struggles with heavy accents or overlapping speech
  • AI summaries capture key decisions, action items, and questions in a digestible format
  • Search works across your entire meeting history — type 'budget decision' and find the exact conversation
  • Integrates with Slack, Notion, Salesforce, and Asana for pushing notes into your workflow automatically

Cons

  • Free tier is limited to 3 meetings total — not enough for a proper evaluation
  • Transcription accuracy varies significantly with background noise, speaker overlap, or accents
  • Privacy concerns about having an AI bot in every meeting can unsettle team members or clients
  • Action item detection is useful but not reliable enough to trust completely; you'll still need to verify
  • The web app can be slow with large meeting histories; searching across 500+ recordings lags
Try Fireflies.ai →

Which Should You Choose?

Both ClickUp and Fireflies.ai are excellent tools in their category. The right choice depends on your specific needs:

  • Choose ClickUp if: ClickUp is the project management tool that tries to be everything for everyone. It has more views and features than any competitor — docs, whiteboards, goals, mind maps, chat, email, even a built-in clip-on timer. The ambitious scope is both its greatest strength and its biggest weakness. If you can tame it, it's incredibly powerful.
  • Choose Fireflies.ai if: Fireflies is the AI meeting assistant that joins your calls, records everything, transcribes the conversation, and generates summaries. It works with Zoom, Google Meet, Teams, and Webex — and it's gotten good enough that you'll stop taking notes entirely. The searchable history is the killer feature: you can find any decision, commitment, or name mentioned across hundreds of past meetings.
We may earn a commission if you purchase through links. All opinions are our own.
